title: What is the difference between Map and Object in JavaScript?
description: When should you use Map over Object?
options: Map: any key type, ordered, has .size, iterable, better for frequent additions/deletions. Object: string/symbol keys only, has prototype chain, works with JSON. Use Map for dictionaries, Object for structured data
correct_answer: Map: any key type, ordered, has .size, iterable, better for frequent additions/deletions. Object: string/symbol keys only, has prototype chain, works with JSON. Use Map for dictionaries, Object for structured data

title: What is the Intl API in JavaScript?
description: How do you use internationalization in JavaScript?
options: Built-in API for locale-aware formatting: Intl.NumberFormat for numbers/currency, Intl.DateTimeFormat for dates, Intl.Collator for string comparison, Intl.RelativeTimeFormat for relative time, Intl.PluralRules
correct_answer: Built-in API for locale-aware formatting: Intl.NumberFormat for numbers/currency, Intl.DateTimeFormat for dates, Intl.Collator for string comparison, Intl.RelativeTimeFormat for relative time, Intl.PluralRules

title: What is the difference between Array.from and spread operator?
options: Array.from() works on array-likes AND iterables, accepts a mapping function as 2nd arg; spread [...x] only works on iterables. Array.from({length:3}, (_, i) => i) creates [0,1,2]; spread can't do this
correct_answer: Array.from() works on array-likes AND iterables, accepts a mapping function as 2nd arg; spread [...x] only works on iterables. Array.from({length:3}, (_, i) => i) creates [0,1,2]; spread can't do this

title: Implement a JavaScript rate limiter.
description: Create a function that limits how often a callback executes.

title: What is the difference between setTimeout(fn, 0) and queueMicrotask?
description: How do these differ in the event loop?
options: queueMicrotask runs before the next macrotask (after current synchronous code), same priority as Promise.then. setTimeout(fn, 0) schedules a macrotask with minimum ~4ms delay. Microtasks always execute before the next render/timer
They run at the same time
correct_answer: queueMicrotask runs before the next macrotask (after current synchronous code), same priority as Promise.then. setTimeout(fn, 0) schedules a macrotask with minimum ~4ms delay. Microtasks always execute before the next render/timer

title: Implement a JavaScript task scheduler with priorities.
What is the Broadcast Channel API?
description: How do you communicate between browser tabs?
options: BroadcastChannel enables simple pub/sub messaging between same-origin windows, tabs, iframes, and workers. new BroadcastChannel('name').postMessage(data); listen with onmessage. Alternative to localStorage events
correct_answer: BroadcastChannel enables simple pub/sub messaging between same-origin windows, tabs, iframes, and workers. new BroadcastChannel('name').postMessage(data); listen with onmessage. Alternative to localStorage events

title: Implement a JavaScript promise pool with concurrency limit.
description: Create a function that runs promises with limited concurrency.

title: What is the Performance API in JavaScript?
description: How do you measure performance in the browser?
options: performance.now() provides microsecond timestamps; performance.mark() creates named timestamps; performance.measure() calculates duration between marks; PerformanceObserver watches for entries. Used for RUM metrics and profiling
correct_answer: performance.now() provides microsecond timestamps; performance.mark() creates named timestamps; performance.measure() calculates duration between marks; PerformanceObserver watches for entries. Used for RUM metrics and profiling

title: Implement a JavaScript virtual DOM diff algorithm.
description: Create a simple VDOM diffing function.

title: What is the Web Crypto API?
description: How do you perform cryptographic operations in JavaScript?
options: crypto.subtle provides async methods: generateKey(), encrypt(), decrypt(), sign(), verify(), digest(). Supports AES, RSA, ECDSA, HMAC, PBKDF2, SHA algorithms. Requires HTTPS context. More secure than JS crypto libraries
correct_answer: crypto.subtle provides async methods: generateKey(), encrypt(), decrypt(), sign(), verify(), digest(). Supports AES, RSA, ECDSA, HMAC, PBKDF2, SHA algorithms. Requires HTTPS context. More secure than JS crypto libraries

title: Implement a JavaScript dependency injection container.
What are JavaScript decorators (Stage 3 proposal)?
description: How will decorators work in JavaScript?
options: @decorator syntax for classes and class members; decorator function receives value and context, returns replacement or undefined. Enables cross-cutting concerns like logging, access control, memoization declaratively. Already in TypeScript 5+
correct_answer: @decorator syntax for classes and class members; decorator function receives value and context, returns replacement or undefined. Enables cross-cutting concerns like logging, access control, memoization declaratively. Already in TypeScript 5+

title: Implement a JavaScript event delegation handler.
description: Create an efficient event delegation system.

title: What is the ResizeObserver API?
options: Observes changes to element dimensions; callback receives ResizeObserverEntry with contentRect, borderBoxSize, contentBoxSize. More efficient than polling or window resize events for responsive components
correct_answer: Observes changes to element dimensions; callback receives ResizeObserverEntry with contentRect, borderBoxSize, contentBoxSize. More efficient than polling or window resize events for responsive components

description: Create a middleware pattern like Express.
